provenance.source_corpus: Wetland Vegetation Monitoring in Cootes Paradise: Measuring the Response to a Fishway/Carp Barrier

source_spans.text: The fishway/carp barrier (Figure 2) was constructed in 1996 and was operational for the 1997 season.
We credit the significant reduction of carp in Cootes Paradise with enhancing the response of emergent vegetation to favorable water levels.
With three years of post-exclusion data we have found that there has indeed been a significant positive response, including increases in plant density and diversity
reductions in suspended sediment and dissolved phosphorus
We attribute the increase in the area of emergent vegetation to three things: water levels, carp exclusion, and planting programs.
The low water levels in 1998 and 1999 allowed emergent vegetation to increase dramatically from natural sources.
The transect surveys show a tremendous increase in submergent densities
The benefits of the planting included increased community awareness
The loss of planted material to resident populations of Canada geese suggested that these animals may be inhibiting the recovery of the vegetation in general.
We have also tried to accelerate the natural recovery of plant communities with a planting program.
